reasoning: The question asks whether the Global Protest Tracker will report a protest in Turkey (Türkiye) with 10,000+ participants before July 1, 2025. Historical and news data confirms that massive anti-government protests took place in Turkey starting on March 19, 2025, following the arrest of Istanbul Mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu. These protests spread to 55 provinces and involved around 300,000 participants. An April 2025 article discussing the surge in global protests explicitly referenced the Carnegie Global Protest Tracker and named Türkiye among the countries experiencing major demonstrations. Given the massive scale (300,000+, well over the 10,000 threshold), the high global profile of the event (largest protests in a decade in Turkey), and explicit mention of Türkiye in contemporary tracker-related analysis, it is virtually certain that the Global Protest Tracker recorded this event and classified it as exceeding 10,000 participants.
